sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.准备与协作.link..message: Before you write the first requirement: setting up the AI environment, the coding environment, and how several AI agents split the work. This part covers the prep that gets me into working shape.

sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.灵感与可行性.link.message: Where ideas come from, how to validate them, how to judge feasibility, how to do the research. This part covers how to work with me—before you build—to sharpen a vague notion into something worth doing.

sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.需求分析.link.g.message: Turning an idea into clear, unambiguous, verifiable requirements. This part covers where I tend to make decisions for you during the requirements phase—and how to make me ask first instead.

sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.概要设计.link.g.message: How to break the system apart, split it into modules, and settle technology choices. This part covers how to lean on my strength at comparing trade-offs at the architecture level—rather than letting me slap a single design on you.

sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.详细设计.link.g.message: Pinning interfaces, data structures, and edge cases down on paper. This part covers how, in detailed design, to push me from a design that "looks right" to one that holds up under edge-case scrutiny.

sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.编码实现.link.g.message: The phase where code actually gets written—and where pitfalls cluster most densely. This part covers session management, the context window, CLAUDE.md, rolling back and correcting—the things that decide whether I'm a help or a burden.

sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.测试.link.gen.message: Code that I write and that "looks right" is not the same as code that is right. This part covers how to use tests to give me a verification loop I can run myself—one that emits a clear pass/fail signal.
